summary: Iteration 59 — Verified (a) cross-page focus-restore feature (useFocusRestore hook + global click/keydown bookmark listener) end-to-end on Library page, including stale-purge gate, bookmark write-on-click, restore-after-Back, and bookmark clear-after-restore. (b) Kotlin compile fix in InstallProgressDialog.kt line 161 (`setLineSpacing(dp(2).toFloat(), 1.0f)`) verified by static grep — no `lineSpacingExtra =` or `lineSpacingMultiplier =` direct assignment in any Kotlin file under /app/android/onnowtv-launcher/. (c) Iteration-57 build-id pill suite (12) + iteration-58 networks/trailer suite (11) all 23 still pass.

android_static_review.evidence: grep -rn 'lineSpacingExtra\s*=\|lineSpacingMultiplier\s*=' /app/android/onnowtv-launcher/app/src/main/java/ → empty. The only `lineSpacingExtra` hits are in /res/layout/activity_main.xml (XML attribute — handled by Android resource compiler, not Kotlin synthetic setter, so SAFE).

additional_finding.issue: TextView.setLineHeight(int) requires API 28+. minSdk is 23. This COMPILES fine (target API 34 JAR has the setter) but will throw NoSuchMethodError at runtime on Android 6.0-8.1 boxes (API 23-27). Not the bug that broke CI, but a latent runtime crash for older TV boxes.
additional_finding.fix: Wrap with `if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= 28) lineHeight = dp(20)` OR call `setLineSpacing(extra, multiplier)` instead.

focus_restore_e2e.evidence_listener: Synthetic dispatch on /profiles wrote `vesper:focus:/profiles` bookmark — listener is live at App boot before user auth.

staleness_gate.evidence: Manually wrote ts=Date.now()-2h to sessionStorage['vesper:focus:/library'], navigated to /library, bookmark removed without restoration.

focus_restore_e2e.network_page_test: PARTIAL — direct page.goto('/networks/binge') was bounced to /profiles by the app's profile gate. Hook is wired and imports verified by code review (line 56 of Network.jsx). Manual on-device verification recommended.

action_items: [OPTIONAL/MEDIUM] BulkInstallActivity.kt:126 + :181 — wrap `lineHeight = dp(N)` with API 28+ guard or use setLineSpacing. Not the cause of the iteration-59 CI failure but will crash on API 23-27 TV boxes at runtime.

[INFO ONLY] Network page focus-restore could not be E2E-tested in preview pod because /networks/binge requires an active profile and direct goto was bounced — code wiring verified; recommend user verify on-device once v2.10.83 lands.
critical_code_review_comments: useFocusRestore.js architecture is clean: capture-phase listener installed once via _installed guard prevents duplicate listeners across remounts. RESTORE_WINDOW_MS=2200 + 80ms tick gives ~27 retries which is generous for slow data loads.
Bookmark is keyed by pathname, so /networks/binge and /networks/disney get separate bookmarks — good.
1-hour staleness gate is sensible and verified to purge.
captureScrollChain walks ancestor scroll containers and only stores those with a stable selector (data-testid/id/data-shelf-rail) — gracefully degrades if no selector found.
Restoration calls scrollIntoView({block:'center', inline:'center'}) after applyScrollChain — correct order so the highlighted tile is centered AFTER container scroll positions land.
App.js uses require() inside useEffect to dodge circular imports — pragmatic given the hook depends on react-router-dom.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Library E2E full pass; Network page wiring verified statically)

seed_data_creation: Seeded test profile via localStorage: onnowtv-profiles-v1:testuser + onnowtv-active-profile-v1:testuser → 'p-test1' Tester.
context_for_next_testing_agent: useFocusRestore hook is solid. Profile gate in preview pod requires post-login localStorage seed with `:testuser` suffix on profile keys (lib/profiles.js line 53). Use window.history.pushState + popstate event for in-app navigation — page.goto() may be intercepted by route guards.
rca_of_the_kotlin_issue: Kotlin synthetic property assignment `view.lineSpacingExtra = X` requires both getter+setter named consistently. Android TextView has `getLineSpacingExtra()` but the setter is `setLineSpacing(float add, float multiplier)` — two args, different name. Kotlin can't synthesize `set` from this, hence the compile error `Val cannot be reassigned`. Fix: call the Java setter directly with both args, which is exactly what line 161 now does.
